div.h2: Identifier Scanning Pipeline: FSM → Keyword Lookup Table → Token Type

p.em: The FSM sees only character classes — the lookup table is what makes a string a keyword

g.text: first ch | already consumed by advance()
FULL lexeme accumulated BEFORE any lookup
FSM sees only char classes, NOT reserved words
Why dict.get() is the right primitive
O(1) hash lookup — no linear scan
Adding keyword = adding ONE dict entry
Grammar never sees raw chars — only TokenType
BROKEN: check keyword table at each char
Parser breaks: 'if fy' is not valid syntax
Invariant: lookup ONLY after while-loop exits
What each stage is responsible for
FSM | Is this sequence of chars an identifier?
Table | Is this identifier a reserved word?
Parser | Is this token valid in this grammar position?
Sema | Does this name refer to a declared symbol?

text.tspan: # NO keyword check yet — FSM is blind to meaning
# NO keyword check yet — FSM is blind to meaning
